summary: Iteration 5 verified the fixed-column Jar rewrite and wild-animal cheer. All six critical review scenarios pass: (1) NO HORIZONTAL MOVEMENT — with 75 seeded poms and one completion, all 9 distinct column x-coordinates remain identical (252,281,310,339,368,397,426,455,484) and the column count-diff was +1 at exactly one column with zero deltas elsewhere. (2) Stratification is correct — average tops: momentum=530.25 > consistency=426.72 > joy=369.3 and the entire bottom row (9 poms) is momentum-colored. This also implicitly confirms momentum-completed poms end in the bottom band and joy-completed poms end near the top (visually confirmed via screenshot). (3) jar-progress reads '75 / 100 poms' and '100 / 100 poms' — GOAL=100 confirmed. (4) Woodland cheer overlay: emoji is one of the wild set 🦊🦌🐿️🦫🦔🐇🦡🦨 (badger/skunk/hedgehog observed), NO dog/cat, NO pom-pom circles inside overlay, overlay is fixed inset-0 full-viewport, critter x decreases (1590 -> 1141) confirming right→left motion. (5) 100-fill flow: pj-jar-glow applied, parade overlay appears, celebration-card shows 'You filled your jar.' + 'New hiking boots', place-on-shelf-button creates a shelf-jar element ('Jul 2026 · New hiking boots'), progress resets to '0 / 100 poms', reward input cleared, glow removed. Zero console errors during all animations.
critical_code_review_comments: Jar.jsx line 91: `left: OFF_X + p.col * STEP` is set in the static `style` prop (never animated) — this is exactly what guarantees no horizontal movement. Confirmed working.

Jar.jsx line 29 `cols[i % PERROW].push(p)` gives each pom a permanent column based on insertion order — deterministic and independent of category, which is why horizontal position is stable across category changes.
WoodlandCheer.jsx uses `initial x:'116vw' -> animate x:'-22vw'` with linear ease — direction and off-screen offsets look good; no pom-poms present in DOM.
Minor: pom color detection in tests depends on inline-style gradient string (browser normalises hex→rgb). No app change needed; documented for future test agents.
seed_data_creation: Seeded localStorage['pomjar_poms'] with combinations of momentum/consistency/joy poms via page.evaluate + reload. Also seeded 99 poms + reward 'New hiking boots' for fill-flow test. All seeds cleared between tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Key gotcha when writing pom-diff tests: when a new pom is added to a column, EVERY pom in that column shifts vertically to make room, so identifying the 'new' pom by (left,top) diff against the before-set will match multiple poms — not just the newly added one. Better strategies: (a) rely on aggregate stratification checks (avg-top per color), (b) tag seeded poms via localStorage IDs and query by data attributes if the component exposed them, or (c) find the pom whose left matches the added column AND that has the maximum top (bottom of column) for a momentum-completed pom. Inline pom color is a CSS radial-gradient string; browsers convert hex→rgb, so match on 'rgb(190, 18, 60)' (momentum), 'rgb(77, 124, 15)' (consistency), 'rgb(234, 179, 8)' (joy).
